frameworks.name: finite element method electric field modeling
frameworks.one_line: Computational head models predict TDCS electric fields from MRI anatomy.

frameworks.name: high-definition TDCS (4x1 and bipolar HD)
frameworks.one_line: Focal montages contrasted with conventional large-electrode TDCS.

core_question: Are more focal TDCS montages more susceptible to interindividual anatomical variability in induced electric fields?
key_assumptions: Individual MRI-based head models faithfully capture relevant anatomy
Electric field magnitude is the relevant dosimetric outcome
Focality and variability are separable montage properties
manipulations: Electrode size in M1-contralateral forehead montage
Montage type: conventional vs 4x1 HD vs bipolar HD
key_findings: Interindividual EF variability increased with focality for conventional and 4x1 HD montages
4x1 HD-TDCS had highest focality and greatest variability
Bipolar HD montages broke the trend: comparable magnitude, slightly lower focality, lower variability
interpretations: Focality in TDCS comes at the cost of increased interindividual variability
Bipolar HD offers a favorable focality-variability trade-off
